mission.title: envoy: Allow loadable modules
mission.description: Allow loadable modules. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
In general, everywhere we use static registration (filters, stats sinks, loggers, tracers, etc.) it would not be difficult to also support dynamic loading.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow loadable modules
steps.description:
Commit Message: dynamic_modules: adds initial object loading logic
Additional Description:
This is the very first commit of the dynamic loading feature discussed among community
members. This is the effort to upstream the playground repository 
 https://github.com/mathetake/envoy-dynamic-modules as an Envoy core extension.
Series of commits will follow this little by little.

#2053, #24230, #32502

Risk Level: N/A (not compiled into the final build yet)
Testing: unit
Docs Changes: N/A
Release

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow loadable modules" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Commit Message: dynamic_modules: adds initial object loading logic
Additional Description:
This is the very first commit of the dynamic loading feature discussed among community
members. This is the effort to upstream the playground repository 
 https://github.com/mathetake/envoy-dynamic-modules as an Envoy core extension.
Series of commits will follow this little by little.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
